north_star: Turn one uploaded book into a clean, organized, evidence-backed learning engine with a knowledge graph.
hard_rules: Do not optimize for a chatbot before the extracted book is clean.
Do not let retrieval hide extraction failures.
Do not silently drop PDF content.
Do not treat LLM output as ground truth without provenance and review.
Do not widen to many books before one book passes Phase 1 gates.
Do not spend expensive model calls without recording why the model was chosen.
Do not commit, document, or log API keys.
Do not choose OpenAI models silently; model choice must be explicit before model-assisted extraction or review.
Do not use Markdown as the durable project documentation format, except README.md as a GitHub-facing adapter.
Do not call Phase 1 outputs canonical until they pass audit, comparison, and review.
Do not hide review corrections inside detector logic; store durable corrections as tracked review override artifacts under reviews/.

Do not adopt paragraph merge policy changes from heuristic warning improvement alone; require gold-set improvement, preserved audit safety metrics, and side-effect review for proposed joins or splits.
Stage, commit, and push meaningful checkpoints when the repo state should be preserved.

phase1_candidate_first_rule.rule: No Phase 1 output should be called canonical until it has passed an audit step.

canonical_promotion_rule.meaning: Canonical means evidence-bound, audited enough under current rules, and safe for downstream use; it does not mean flawless.
canonical_promotion_rule.review_gate: Review canonical_paragraphs.jsonl with canonical_paragraph_review_report.json before downstream use, including warning drilldowns, risk clusters, bbox span diagnostics and decision summaries when span risk dominates, merge experiment reports before adopting merge policy changes, merge failure taxonomy after failed merge experiments, gold evaluation reports before any merge-policy adoption, cross-page join review reports and curated join decisions before cross-page continuation adoption, policy_adoption_decision.json after adoption, post_adoption_canonical_safety_report.json after active-policy changes, post_adoption_bbox_span_diagnosis.json before changing bbox/span rules, post_adoption_remediation_plan.json before choosing a fix class, front_matter_metadata_review_report.json before front-matter promotion changes, visual_review_cases_report.json before grouping changes, narrow_grouping_correction_design.json before implementing a grouping correction, chained_cross_page_continuation_experiment.json before chained-continuation adoption, chained_join_review_queue.json before reviewing v3 side effects, and top corrective recommendation. Do not adopt paragraph-break or cross-page continuation policy experiments unless they improve against authoritative gold review rows, preserve audit safety metrics, avoid unacceptable over-splitting or over-merging, and have joined examples reviewed. For Douglass phase1_v3, v2_cross_page_continuation is active, but downstream intelligence remains blocked until canonical paragraph review becomes safe. The gold-set gap queue has been expanded to 0. The front-matter/metadata review found 7 valid Preface/Letter paragraphs and 2 valid Chapter I body paragraphs caught by an overbroad early-page warning. The visual-review queue found 7 valid canonical paragraphs and 1 true grouping defect on page 109. The v3 chained-continuation experiment fixed cp_000103 and improved paragraph precision/recall to 1.000, but it proposed 9 chained joins with 8 outside authoritative gold coverage. The chained review queue classifies those 8 as 5 likely valid continuations, 2 needs visual review, and 1 structure-boundary risk, so v3 is not active and needs side-effect review before adoption.

gold_review_rule.adoption_rule: No paragraph merge policy may become active merely because heuristic warning counts improve. It must improve against authoritative gold review rows and must not worsen audit safety metrics.
gold_review_rule.seed_policy: Seed/example rows help establish workflow but are excluded from scoring until marked authoritative after visual review.

cross_page_join_decision_rule.adoption_rule: Cross-page continuation cannot become active while boundary, structure, manual-review, invalid, duplicate, or mismatched join decisions remain unresolved.

start: Read current Phase 1 strategy and artifact contract.
Read the latest relevant history entry.
Append an observability event at the start of meaningful work, before the first edit.
closure: Run python3 -m pytest tests before committing code or artifact-contract changes.
If pytest is unavailable, use the direct test-function runner only as a temporary fallback and document the limitation.
Update history, lessons, or strategy when the result changes project knowledge.

Before adopting paragraph merge policy changes, verify gold_evaluation_report.json shows authoritative gold-set improvement, no audit safety regression, and side-effect review for proposed joins or splits.
Append an observability event summarizing the outcome.
Stage, commit, and push meaningful durable changes.

verification.fallback_policy: Direct test-function execution is allowed only as a temporary fallback when pytest is unavailable; document the fallback.

formatting_rules.local_html_docs: Use the standard light document style in docs/.
formatting_rules.dark_mode_scope: Reserve dark mode for runtime observability surfaces, not ordinary project documentation.
formatting_rules.github_readme_scope: README.md is a GitHub-facing adapter, not the durable local source of truth.

observability.port_rule: Reuse the same observability port. Do not open new ports for each build.
